data.title: Oak Woods Acquisition receives Nasdaq notice over annual meeting requirement
data.description:
• Oak Woods (Nasdaq: OAKU) on Wednesday said that on January 16, 2026, it received a notice from Nasdaq stating the company is not in compliance with listing rules requiring an annual meeting of shareholders.
  • The company said it failed to hold an annual meeting within 12 months of the end of its fiscal year, breaching Nasdaq Listing Rule 5620(a).
  • The notice has no immediate impact on the listing or trading of its securities.
  • Oak Woods said it has 45 days, until March 2, to submit a plan to regain compliance. If accepted, Nasdaq may grant the company up to June 29 to hold an annual meeting and regain compliance.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: American Rebel approves 1-for-20 reverse stock split
data.description:
• American Rebel (AREB) on Wednesday announced that it will effect a 1-for-20 reverse stock split of its common stock, effective on Feb. 2, 2026.
  • The reverse stock split is intended to increase the share price, enhance marketability, and maintain compliance with Nasdaq’s $1 minimum bid price requirement.
  • No fractional shares will be issued, with fractions rounded up to the nearest whole share, and shareholders holding 100 or more shares will not be reduced below 100 shares.
  • The transaction will reduce shares outstanding to approximately 0.44M from about 8.7M as of Jan. 20, 2026.
  • The company said on Nov. 25, 2025, stockholders holding most common and Series A preferred shares approved a 1-for-25 reverse stock split, which became effective Jan. 5, 2026.
  • Shares -7.68%.

data.title: Powell will be very unhappy if he stays on the Fed, Trump says
data.description:
If Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell decides to stay on the Fed board even after his term as chair ends, "his life won't be very, very happy, I don't think, by doing that," President Donald Trump said in a televised interview on Wednesday. "I think he wants to get out. He has not done a good job."

The president lambasted him, once again, for being too late on lowering interest rates in an interview on CNBC. He also chastised him for the cost overruns at the Fed's renovation project. "I could have fixed it for 25M bucks. It would have looked beautiful," Trump said.  "I think he's up to almost $4B." (The renovation project is at $2.5B, Powell told senators in a letter last week.)

He also said he's narrowing down the number of candidates for the Federal Reserve chair. "I would say we're down to three, but we're down to two, and I could probably tell you we're down to maybe one in my mind," Trump said. He repeated that he would prefer to keep National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett in his current role.

The other potential nominees include former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh, current Fed Governor Christopher Waller, and BlackRock's Rick Rieder.  "All three are good," Trump said. "We have a problem with the man that's there now."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: December home sales climb 15% M/M as normalization continues: Re/Max
data.description:
U.S. home sales advanced 14.9% M/M in December as inventory retreated, Re/Max Holdings (RMAX) said in a report on Wednesday. Sales gained 5% from a year earlier, while supply grew 16.5% Y/Y.

The number of homes for sale last month fell 11.0% from November, and new listings dropped 23.2% sequentially, amid lower mortgage rates. 

"The December housing market continued to show signs of normalization," Re/Max CEO Erik Carlson said in a statement. Inventory rose, prices leveled and activity remained resilient – all indicators of a market finding healthier footing."

In addition, Re/Max (RMAX) said, the average days on market increased to 61 days, a full week longer than what was logged in November 2025 and December 2024 and the longest average listing-to-closing period in at least four years.

The median sales price of $434K rose 1.1% from a year ago.

The REMAX National Housing Report is based on MLS data for the stated month in 52 metropolitan areas, including single-family residential property types.

data.title: Yelp to acquire AI lead management platform Hatch
data.description:
Yelp (YELP) on Wednesday said it would acquire AI-powered lead management and communication platform Hatch.

Under the terms of the agreement, Yelp will acquire Hatch for about $270 million in cash with an additional $30 million of employee retention to be paid out over two to three years.

San Francisco, California-based Yelp said that the acquisition would help the company to advance its AI transformation and expand SaaS offerings.

“The acquisition of Hatch is an important step forward in Yelp’s AI transformation, accelerating our strategy to bring powerful new AI tools to local businesses,” said Yelp CEO Jeremy Stoppelman.

Hatch's AI-powered customer communication platform helps businesses increase conversion and retention at lower overhead costs. As of November 2025, Hatch had achieved about $25 million in annual recurring revenue.

The transaction is expected to close in early February, Yelp said. Upon the closing of the deal, Hatch will be a wholly owned subsidiary of Yelp.

data.title: Knight-Swift misses Q4 results but sees capacity returning to normal
data.description:
Knight-Swift’s (KNX) fourth-quarter results reflected both the lack of the typical seasonal lift in demand late in the year and a large impairment change, initially driving shares sharply lower in after-hours trading.

The stock has recovered some of its initial loss, however, on the company’s assurances that recent government efforts to prevent and revoke invalidly issued CDLs and shut down non-compliant CDL schools will help restore capacity to healthier levels and enable carriers to recover cost inflation and restore margins.

“In the [less than truckload] market, prevailing demand levels declined noticeably in October and remained soft throughout the quarter. In early January, LTL demand appears to be off to a better start than what we saw in the fourth quarter, and bid discussions continue to be encouraging. We remain focused on controlling our costs, delivering quality service, and growing into our expanded network,” Knight-Swift CEO Adam Miller said.

While the LTL segment and “other segments” (which include independent contractors and third-party carriers) saw an increase of  7% and 17.6% in sales for the quarter, respectively, the gain was more than offset by declines in truckload (-2.4%), logistics (-4.8%), and intermodal (-3.4%), resulting in a 0.4% decline in total revenue to $1.86M, missing expectations by $40M.

On an unadjusted basis—which includes a $52.9M impairment charge—Knight -- Knight-Swift (KNX) swung to a loss of $0.04 per share versus a profit of $0.43 a year ago and against estimates for a profit of $0.30. Adjusted income was down 5 cents from a year ago to $0.31 per share, 4 cents below expectations.

By segment, LTL saw a 4.8% decline in operating income that was offset by a 7% increase in revenue, with the segment’s adjusted operating ratio widening 60 basis points to 95.1%.

In truckload, revenue was down 2.4% and adjusted operating income fell by 10.7%, while the adjusted operating ratio improved 70 basis points to 92.9%.

Sales in the logistics sector were down 4.8%, adjusted operating income was down 36.6%, and adjusted operating ratio was up 210 basis points to 95.8%, while in intermodal, revenue was down 3.4%, and operating loss narrowed 93%, leading to a compressed operating ratio of 100.1%.

Looking ahead to the current quarter, the company expects to earn a profit between $0.28 and $0.32 per share, with a midpoint of $0.30, a penny short of expectations. These estimates include the assumptions that revenue will be down slightly for truckload and down low single digits for logistics, and up 5% to 10% for LTL.

Shares are back in the red, trading more than 3% lower in after-hour trading.

data.title: WhiteFiber plans a $200M private placement of convertible senior notes
data.description:
• WhiteFiber (WYFI) announced  on Wednesday that it plans a $200M private placement of convertible senior notes due 2031, with an option for initial purchasers to buy up to an additional $30M.
  • The company will have the option to deliver cash, ordinary shares, or both to settle note conversions.
  • Proceeds will be used to fund a zero-strike call option and mainly for center expansion, including new facilities, energy agreements, and equipment, with the balance for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • Pursuant to the zero-strike call option transaction, the company will pay a premium for the right to receive ordinary shares at expiry or early settlement.
  • This structure is intended to facilitate hedging by note investors, and that may affect the share price.
  • Shares +6.00%.
